articles.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
articles.description:
Last month, OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool, and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company. OpenAI has been shifting its priorities as part of an effort to avoid "side quests," and Peebles' departure is just one of many recent changes as the company moves to focus more on coding and enterprise use.
As part of a note Peebles posted on X, he said:
I am immensely grateful to Sam, Mark, Aditya and Jakub for fostering a research environment that allowed us to pursue ideas off-the-beaten path from the company's mainline roadmap. It's tempting in life to mode collapse to the most i …

Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Air Canada temporarily suspends some flights to New York and other locations
articles.description:
Spirit Airlines reportedly seeks emergency US government funding as war against Iran keeps aviation fuel costs high

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Air Canada has announced a temporary suspension of flights from Toronto and Montreal to New York’s John F Kennedy airport, citing rising fuel prices.
The move comes amid growing concerns that airlines worldwide may scale back services as aviation fuel costs climb in the wake of the US and Israel’s ongoing war with Iran, which entered a fragile ceasefire earlier in April. Although Iran announced on Friday that the strait of Hormuz had reopened, helping ease oil prices, fuel costs remain significantly elevated after weeks of disruption.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
articles.description:
Tinder users who prove they're a real person by visiting an identity-verifying orb will soon be able to get five free boosts in the app - and it's just the latest service to embrace the orb. World, which was co-founded by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, initially tested Tinder verification using its facial scanning orbs through a pilot program in Japan last year. It's now expanding the service to "select markets, including Japan and the United States." 
To verify that they're not a bot or an AI agent, users have to physically visit one of World's orbs in person. According to World, the orb "takes pictures of your face and eyes, then encrypts and sto …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
articles.description:
The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It's dubbed the company a "RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY" full of "Leftwing nut jobs" and a menace to national security. But some of the ice may reportedly be melting between the two, thanks to Anthropic's buzzy new cybersecurity-focused model: Claude Mythos Preview.
Anthropic's relationship with the Pentagon soured quickly in late February after the company refused to budge on two red lines: using its technology for domestic mass surveillance or lethal fully autonomous weapons with no human in the loop. Anthropic's tech has in the past been used heavily b …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Parliament adjourned sine die, Budget session concludes
articles.description: As soon as the House met at 11 a.m., Speaker Om Birla read out his concluding remarks, following which he adjourned the House sine die (for an indefinite period)

articles.title: Parliament special session: Both Houses adjourned sine die, Budget session concludes
articles.description: The Budget Session of Parliament concluded sine die after the women's reservation Bill failed to pass in the Lok Sabha, falling short of the required two-thirds majority. The government also deferred the contentious delimitation proposal amid Opposition pushback, ending the session on a politically charged note.

articles.title: Parliament special sitting LIVE: Parliament adjourned sine die as Budget session concludes
articles.description: Priyanka Gandhi challenged govt to bring back old Women's Bill; leader of the Opposition Rahul Gandhi had accused the government of “hiding behind women” to alter the country’s electoral map.

articles.title: Relocation fear returns to haunt Amrabad’s particularly vulnerable Chenchu tribe
articles.description: It is a common practice among Chenchus to establish ‘pentas’  (habitation) wherever they move to in search of food

articles.title: Maximum temperatures in 10 Telangana districts cross 44℃, all districts above 42℃
articles.description: Dasturabad in Nirmal district emerged as the hottest location, recording a maximum temperature of 44.5℃

articles.title: RCB vs DC prediction: Who’ll win, Bengaluru or Delhi? Grok, ChatGPT, and Google Gemini have no confusion
articles.description: Grok, ChatGPT and Google Gemini predict the winner of the Royal Challengers Bengaluru vs Delhi Capitals match in the IPL 2026. The AI tools have no confusion about who'll win today.

articles.title: When India's secret naval blockade forced Pakistan to surrender
articles.description: When Indias secret naval blockade forced Pakistan to surrender

articles.title: Higher education budget up by 60% in 5 years, yet T.N. govt. universities continue to struggle for funds
articles.description: A State government committee noted in its October 2025 report that the existing model of disbursement of grants has not resulted in excellence in academics or better financial discipline

articles.title: Rodrigues: 'When time came to capitalise, we were not able to'
articles.description: "In the next match, I will come back and take that responsibility again," Jemimah Rodrigues says after India lost the first T20I to South Africa

articles.title: DMK must return to power as they consistently deliver on promises, says Kamal
articles.description: Opposition to Delimitation Bill in Parliament is a fight against forces that tried to stifle the State, says the MNM leader; he says DMK govt. is truthful and honest, and did not play with people’s lives

topStories.title: Trump says US will ‘start dropping bombs again’ if no Iran deal is reached
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump says the US will “have to start dropping bombs again” if no US-Iran deal is reached.

topStories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 50 of the US-Iran conflict?
topStories.description: Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz with conditions and threatens closure if the US blockade of Iranian ports continues.

topStories.title: US, Iran Prepare For Second Round Of Direct Talks In Pakistan On April 20
topStories.description: President Trump indicated a second round of direct US-Iran talks could occur Monday in Islamabad as both sides seek a peace deal amid ongoing Middle East conflict.

topStories.title: Quote of the Day by Maya Angelou on freedom: 'The truth is, no one of us can be free…'
topStories.description: Maya Angelou’s quote on freedom shows why no team, company, or leader can truly thrive while others remain excluded from opportunity.

topStories.title: Oil prices well below $100 as Strait of Hormuz reopens but experts aren’t convinced just yet. Here’s why
topStories.description: Global markets are breathing easier as Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz fully open, a crucial oil route, amid a potential U.S.-Iran deal. This development, coupled with a Lebanon-Israel ceasefire, has sent oil prices plummeting. While experts see a temporary reprieve, concerns linger over Iran's nuclear program and sanctions, suggesting oil prices may remain elevated.

topStories.title: "US Will Start Dropping Bombs Again If No Deal Reached": Trump Warns Iran
topStories.description: Direct talks between the US and Iran last weekend were inconclusive, as the two nations could not agree about Iran's nuclear program and other points.

topStories.title: New Jersey announces $150 transit tickets to reach the World Cup
topStories.description: Transit fares from New York to MetLife Stadium in New Jersey for World Cup matches will rise to $150.

topStories.title: Trump: Xi Pleased By Strait Of Hormuz Reopening, Predicts 'Historic' China Meeting
topStories.description: Iran on Fridayhad said it had fully reopened the Strait of Hormuz to commercial vessels, but questions remained on Saturday over how freely ships could transit the waterway, as Tehran maintained...

topStories.title: Millions under threat of severe weather, including tornadoes
topStories.description: As storms develop Friday afternoon, there is a chance for discrete supercells with very large hail, strong tornadoes and destructive, straight-line winds.

topStories.title: Singer d4vd arrested on suspicion of murder in death of Celeste Rivas, LAPD says
topStories.description: Singer and songwriter d4vd has been arrested on suspicion of murder for the death of a 14-year-old whose dismembered body was found last year in a Tesla belonging to the singer.

topStories.title: Iran disputes claims of new agreements with Trump
topStories.description: Tehran put limits on the Strait of Hormuz’s opening, as the United States issued a new sanctions exemption on the sale of Russian oil.

topStories.title: A Potent Threat in Strait of Hormuz: Iran’s “Mosquito Fleet”
topStories.description: Separate from the regular Iranian Navy, with boats that often go more than 115 miles per hour, it’s what a retired U.S. official calls a “disruptive force.”

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. will continue blockade after Iran says strait "completely open"
dailyNews.description: President Trump has welcomed Iran's announcement that, at least while the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to all ships.

dailyNews.title: Trump and the IRS are in talks to resolve his $10 billion lawsuit over leaked tax records
dailyNews.description: The president's lawsuit, filed this year, alleges that the IRS failed to take the necessary steps to prevent the unauthorized release of his tax documents.

dailyNews.title: Air Force Academy’s ‘CULEX’ puts thousands of cadets through realistic 24-hour combat simulation
dailyNews.description: The CULEX focuses on building confidence, teamwork and leadership skills rather than testing Air Force and Space Force cadets with a pass-or-fail system.

dailyNews.title: Trump plans to ease access to psychedelics like psilocybin, ibogaine
dailyNews.description: The president will direct federal agencies to boost research into the drugs and support clinical trials, moves championed by Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

dailyNews.title: Federal Court Temporarily Freezes Nexstar’s Merger With Tegna
dailyNews.description: The judge said the two television companies could not combine operations while an antitrust lawsuit proceeded. Nexstar said its deal was already done.

dailyNews.title: Trades could save NFL draft lacking franchise QBs, polarizing players from being a yawner
dailyNews.description: The 2026 NFL draft lacks marquee quarterbacks and compelling storylines, but NFL GMs say trades could provide the excitement, with multiple teams holding extra first-round picks.

dailyNews.title: Trump Will Participate in a Marathon Bible Reading
dailyNews.description: He will read a passage from the Old Testament that his Christian supporters cite as a call to national repentance and divine blessing.

dailyNews.title: Reported tornado in Lena, Illinois, causes major damage; town is shut down
dailyNews.description: A reported tornado in Lena, Illinois, about 47 miles west of Rockford, has caused extensive storm damage, including possibly to schools that had students inside at the time.

dailyNews.title: Trump Extends Sanctions Exemption on Some Russian Oil as High Gas Prices Persist
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration made the announcement hours after Iran said that the Strait of Hormuz was open to commercial ships.

dailyNews.title: Disney tacitly admits it made a multi-billion dollar mistake with 'Star Wars' land
dailyNews.description: Disney is finally adding Luke, Leia, and Han Solo to Galaxy’s Edge after years of pretending fans preferred to meet the mediocre sequel trilogy characters.

dailyNews.title: Trump Is Urged to Move on Nuclear Site Thought to Be Beyond Reach of Bombs
dailyNews.description: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.

dailyNews.title: Watch Pope Leo's visit to Cameroon, drawing over 100,000 worshippers
dailyNews.description: Pope Leo XIV visited Cameroon on his tour of Africa. He garnered a crowd of around 120,000 people for mass in Douala. He also spoke with students and faculty at the Catholic University of Central Africa and attended a Catholic hospital. The visit came amid his ongoing feud with President Trump.

dailyNews.title: Trump set to read Scripture from the Oval Office during 'America Reads the Bible' event starting Sunday
dailyNews.description: The president is set to read 2 Chronicles 7 during the 'America Reads the Bible' event, a passage organizers say was deliberately reserved for him.

dailyNews.title: U.S. delegation visited Cuba last week as Trump heaped pressure on island
dailyNews.description: A delegation of senior State Department representatives traveled to Cuba via a U.S. government plane last week, officials said, a diplomatic opening amid intense pressure from the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Dispute With Pope Leo Deepens Divisions on the Right
dailyNews.description: Sean Hannity criticized the pope. Tucker Carlson attacked Mr. Hannity. And President Trump suggested ranking MAGA figures: “good, bad, and somewhere in the middle.”

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: Iran pushes back on Trump's claims about agreement
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.

dailyNews.title: Deaths of migrants in ICE custody hit record high under Trump
dailyNews.description: Twenty-nine people have died in ICE custody since October, the start of the federal government's fiscal year, already surpassing 2004's toll of 28, the previous record, according to government data.

dailyNews.title: Trump came to Arizona to rally young voters. An older crowd greeted him.
dailyNews.description: Despite some empty seats, Trump projected confidence in his party’s ability to win the midterm elections. Some in the crowd focused on GOP divisions.

dailyNews.title: Agencies in talks with Trump, family to resolve $10B lawsuit over tax leaks
dailyNews.description: President Trump and his family are suing federal agencies over leaks of their tax information. Court filings say they’re in talks to find a solution instead of heading to trial.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he had 'no idea' Anthropic's Amodei met with White House about Mythos
dailyNews.description: The meeting comes less than two months after President Donald Trump blacklisted Anthropic's Claude.

dailyNews.title: In Las Vegas, Trump’s economic pitch meets a skeptical audience
dailyNews.description: Trump touts “no tax on tips” in Las Vegas as voters grapple with high prices and economic unease.

dailyNews.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
dailyNews.description:
The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It's dubbed the company a "RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY" full of "Leftwing nut jobs" and a menace to national security. But some of the ice may reportedly be melting between the two, thanks to Anthropic's buzzy new cybersecurity-focused model: Claude Mythos Preview.
Anthropic's relationship with the Pentagon soured quickly in late February after the company refused to budge on two red lines: using its technology for domestic mass surveillance or lethal fully autonomous weapons with no human in the loop. Anthropic's tech has in the past been used heavily b …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Trump picks qualified, normal health leader to head CDC; experts still cautious
dailyNews.description: She's well qualified but will need to navigate RFK Jr.'s anti-vaccine agenda.

dailyNews.title: RFK Jr clashes with Dem lawmaker over Trump’s mental fitness in heated exchange
dailyNews.description: RFK Jr. defended Trump's mental health during a tense congressional hearing, rejecting calls for a fitness assessment or invoking the 25th Amendment.
